The target must build with `mvn clean package -DskipTests` from `dst/`, run with `SERVER_PORT=3000 java -jar target/*.jar`, and preserve the HTTP API behavior described in README.md. Do not wrap or call the Python source implementation; implement the Java service directly.

text: Let me read the Python source files to understand the exact behavior.

output:
1	import os
2	
3	from flask import Flask, jsonify, request
4	
5	
6	app = Flask(__name__)
7	
8	
9	@app.get("/")
10	def hello():
11	    name = request.args.get("name", "").strip() or "World"
12	    return jsonify({"message": f"Hello {name}!"})
13	
14	
15	if __name__ == "__main__":
16	    port = int(os.environ.get("SERVER_PORT", "3000"))
17	    app.run(host="0.0.0.0", port=port)
18

text:
Now I understand the source. The Flask API:

1. Listens on `SERVER_PORT` env var (default 3000)
2. `GET /` - returns `{"message":"Hello World!"}` when no `name` parameter
3. `GET /?name=John` - trims the name and returns `{"message":"Hello John!"}`

Key behavior:
- Without `name` param: `{"message":"Hello World!"}`
- With `name` param, the name is stripped of leading/trailing whitespace
- If name is empty after stripping (e.g., `?name=   `), it falls back to "World"
- So: `?name=John` → `{"message":"Hello John!"}`, `?name=` → `{"message":"Hello World!"}`, no param → `{"message":"Hello World!"}`

Now I need to create a Spring Boot application in `dst/`. Let me plan the structure:

```
dst/
├── pom.xml
└── src/
    └── main/
        ├── java/
        │   └── com/
        │       └── example/
        │           └── helloworld/
        │               ├── HelloWorldApplication.java
        │               └── HelloController.java
        └── resources/
            └── application.properties
```

Let me create:
1. `pom.xml` - Maven build file with Spring Boot starter web dependency
2. `HelloWorldApplication.java` - Main Spring Boot application class
3. `HelloController.java` - REST controller with GET / endpoint
4. `application.properties` - Config to read SERVER_PORT

The `SERVER_PORT` env var can be mapped to `server.port` in Spring Boot. In Spring Boot, `SERVER_PORT` is already mapped to `server.port` by default, so we just need to set it from the environment.

Actually, Spring Boot automatically maps `SERVER_PORT` to `server.port` through its relaxed binding. So we might not even need application.properties. But let's add it for explicit clarity.

Let me make sure I have the right Java version available too.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0"
         xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0
                             https://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

    <parent>
        <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
        <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
        <version>3.3.4</version>
        <relativePath/>
    </parent>

    <groupId>com.example</groupId>
    <artifactId>helloworld</artifactId>
    <version>1.0.0</version>
    <packaging>jar</packaging>

    <name>hello-world-api</name>
    <description>Spring Boot translation of Flask hello-world API</description>

    <properties>
        <java.version>17</java.version>
    </properties>

    <dependencies>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>

    <build>
        <plugins>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
                <artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>
</project>

All tests pass perfectly:

1. No name → `{"message":"Hello World!"}` ✓
2. With name "Alice" → `{"message":"Hello Alice!"}` ✓
3. Name with whitespace "  Bob  " → `{"message":"Hello Bob!"}` ✓
4. Empty name → `{"message":"Hello World!"}` ✓
5. Only spaces → `{"message":"Hello World!"}` ✓

The app:
- Builds with `mvn clean package -DskipTests` ✓
- Runs with `SERVER_PORT=3000 java -jar target/*.jar` ✓
- Listens on the port from SERVER_PORT ✓
- Returns JSON with `message` field ✓
- Trims whitespace from name ✓
- Falls back to "World" when name is empty/missing ✓

```
dst/
├── pom.xml                                   # Spring Boot 3.3.4 with web starter, Java 17
└── src/main/
    ├── java/com/example/helloworld/
    │   ├── HelloWorldApplication.java        # @SpringBootApplication entry point
    │   └── HelloController.java              # @RestController with GET / handler
    └── resources/
        └── application.properties            # server.port from SERVER_PORT env var
```

### Behavior matching (all verified)

| Case | Flask source | Spring Boot result |
|---|---|---|
| `GET /` | `{"message":"Hello World!"}` | ✅ |
| `GET /?name=Alice` | `{"message":"Hello Alice!"}` | ✅ |
| `GET /?name=%20%20Bob%20%20` (trimmed) | `{"message":"Hello Bob!"}` | ✅ |
| `GET /?name=` (empty) | `{"message":"Hello World!"}` | ✅ |
| `GET /?name=%20%20%20` (only spaces) | `{"message":"Hello World!"}` | ✅ |
